motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Overcast and holding still, 19°F. The cold deepened overnight without announcement. The sky is the colour of old envelope paper and makes no promises. By mid-morning a fine dry snow arrived — not the kind that accumulates, but the kind that drifts sideways and disappears against the grey, so that the town cannot quite agree, by noon, whether it snowed or did not snow.

undercurrent.note: The shelving unit has arrived one week early — a logistical fact that is also, this editor notes privately, the first time the external world has moved toward the alcove rather than the alcove moving toward it. The returned have shifted their bearing by eighteen degrees, which Toby has put in the Watch column and in a letter. Agnes has confirmed in print that Calloway already knows when the room is ready. Three convergences in one day: the early delivery, the shifted bearing, the letter. The level climbs to 28. Stage moves to 1: something is off. The town cannot quite yet name what it is watching, but it has begun, in the way of small towns, to watch.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: The library's east alcove has been empty since 2009, when the last cataloguer left in the middle of a Wednesday with no explanation. Calloway has begun shelving it. The books that appear on the new shelves overnight are not books the library owns, and they are not books that have ever been published, but they are about Greywater Falls — and they are set in years that have not happened yet.
